_comment: Illustrative gpt-4o output. Actual recommendations will vary.

brief.planning_brief_title: Q3 2026 Product Planning Brief — Consolidate Enterprise Security, Accelerate Mobile, Selective Integrations

recommended_bets.rationale: Non-negotiable before SOC 2 audit (September 1). RBAC carry-over is the #1 priority. Data retention policy can follow in Q4 if capacity is tight.
45% daily active rate in beta is a rare retention signal. iOS GA is low-effort (beta is proven). Android v1 unlocks the other 50% of the mobile market. Offline mode deferred to Q4.
Enterprise customers are heavily Microsoft. Teams integration unblocks deals. Salesforce and Zapier deferred — lower signal, higher effort.

recommended_initiatives: Address recurring tasks deletion behavior (from Q2 deferral)
20% capacity reserved for bugs and incidents
recommended_bets.rationale: Deferred technical debt compounds. Reserve 10% for unplanned work and resolve the recurring tasks open question in week 1.

tradeoffs: Gantt chart view deferred again — 167 votes but insufficient capacity given SOC 2 deadline. Communicate timeline to users.
Salesforce integration deferred — requires dedicated 4-week engineering effort. Revisit Q4.
Offline mode for mobile deferred — iOS GA is sufficient to capture mobile retention upside this quarter.
Data retention policy deferred to Q4 — RBAC and audit log are the SOC 2 blockers. Retention policy is nice-to-have.
must_do_this_quarter: RBAC — required for SOC 2 audit by September 1
Audit log — required for SOC 2 audit by September 1
iOS GA from beta — low-effort, high-confidence delivery

capacity_allocation.description: 8 engineers over 13 weeks. 40% enterprise security (SOC 2), 35% mobile, 15% integrations, 10% quality/debt.

key_risks.risk: RBAC slips again — 6-week estimate based on Q2 complexity. Any ambiguity in scope re-opens the slip risk.
key_risks.mitigation: Freeze RBAC scope in week 1. EM does daily check-in from week 4.

key_risks.risk: Android v1 discovery — no prior Android codebase exists. Estimate may be optimistic.
key_risks.mitigation: Run a 1-week technical spike in week 1 to validate Android estimate before committing.

key_risks.risk: SOC 2 scope expands — auditor may require additional controls not in current scope.
key_risks.mitigation: Complete SOC 2 readiness review in week 2. Identify and escalate any gaps immediately.

key_risks.risk: CompetitorX launches in mid-market — 6-12 month window may be shorter than expected.
key_risks.mitigation: Track CompetitorX release cadence weekly (workflow #47). Accelerate integrations if they launch.

recommended_okrs.objective: Unblock enterprise sales with a complete security foundation
key_results: RBAC shipped and in production by August 15
Audit log shipped by August 29 (1 week SOC 2 buffer)
3 new enterprise logos closed citing security features

recommended_okrs.objective: Establish mobile as a core product surface
key_results: iOS app reaches GA (stable, out of beta) by July 31
Android v1 shipped to 10 beta customers by September 15
Mobile daily active rate maintained at ≥ 40% (from 45% in Q2 beta)

recommended_okrs.objective: Grow net new ARR by $220k in Q3
